details: Reg A regression on astancov/eltwise_run7_refined HEAD 49a16d65cb5
symptom: 24 fail / 69 pass / 32 skip moreh_softmax_backward w.cpp/w_large.cpp + 1 forward [15,109,64,64]

details: Step 3 of _w_large.cpp SOFTMAX path: Sub(bcast Col) -> Mul(None). init_bcast<ELWSUB,COL> may leave unpacker state that mul_tiles_init does not fully reset. DEVICE_PRINT cb_inter2 (Sub result) and cb_dx (Mul result) to find divergence.

chain helpers BinaryFpu::init() for Bcast!=None invoke init_bcast<>(CbA,CbB,ocb) which is a FULL HW configure (llk_unpack_hw_configure + llk_pack_hw_configure + llk_math_hw_configure + llk_pack_init + llk_pack_dest_init + llk_math_pack_sync_init). Per compute_kernel_hw_startup.h:26-30 mid-MAIN() HW configure is undefined. The raw moreh_common helpers use _short variants (e.g. sub_bcast_cols_init_short) which only call llk_math_eltwise_binary_init_with_operands + llk_unpack_AB_init.

Two-kernel raw-LLK swap (sub_tiles_bcast_cols_to_cb in _w_large.cpp and sub_tiles_bcast_rows_to_cb in _h_large.cpp). Each kernel passes its primary failing shape in isolation. Cross-test pollution remains in --run-all mode — first test passes, subsequent tests in same process fail. This corroborates the diagnosis: chain helper init_bcast<>() is a full HW configure that leaves residual state. Each program does binary_op_init_common at boot, which resets some but apparently NOT ALL HW state. Root cause confirmed: BinaryFpu::init() (eltwise_chain.inl:522) calls full init_bcast<>(CbA,CbB,ocb) when Bcast != None, which is undefined mid-MAIN.

Root cause identified: chain helper init_bcast<>(CbA,CbB,ocb) at eltwise_chain.inl line 522 is a FULL HW configure (calls llk_unpack_hw_configure, llk_pack_hw_configure, llk_math_hw_configure, llk_pack_init, llk_pack_dest_init, llk_math_pack_sync_init). This is invoked PER-TILE for any BinaryFpu chain element with Bcast != None (because BinaryFpu sets clashes_with_fpu=true, forcing emit_init_per_tile=true). Per compute_kernel_hw_startup.h:26-30, mid-MAIN() HW configure is undefined behavior. The original commit f20938cea0f introduced this. Kernel-side falsifier confirms: replacing one bcast chain call with raw _short form makes the test pass. Forward [15,109,64,64] test_softmax_for_dim_nc passes on its own — likely a flaky failure in the briefing (or due to cross-test pollution from neighbor failures). Proposed fix: change helper to use _short form (llk_math_eltwise_binary_init + llk_unpack_AB_init only, NO hw_configure) for Bcast != None case.

Root cause: eltwise_chain.inl:522 BinaryFpu::init() Bcast != None branch calls full init_bcast<>() which emits llk_unpack_hw_configure + llk_pack_hw_configure + llk_math_hw_configure + llk_pack_init + llk_pack_dest_init + llk_math_pack_sync_init. Per compute_kernel_hw_startup.h these MMIO writes are undefined mid-MAIN. BinaryFpu fires per-tile because clashes_with_fpu=true. Raw moreh_common helpers use _short form (math+unpack init only). UnaryBcast::init() (line 704) has identical bug via unary_bcast_init.
proposed_fix: Helper one-point change at eltwise_chain.inl:511-523 to use llk_math_eltwise_binary_init + llk_unpack_AB_init instead of init_bcast<>. ~7 LOC.
constraint: User said helpers FROZEN — kernel-side workaround per callsite is feasible but ~20+ edits across moreh kernels
forward_failure: test_softmax_for_dim_nc[15,109,64,64] runs moreh_softmax_c_large.cpp which uses Bcast=None hardcoded — NOT same root cause. Briefing failure was likely cross-test pollution from neighbor test that does use bcast chain.
